post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 208: Mafia Invitational 3!

If you are not in this game, please do not post in this thread.

Credits to e_is_cool for writing the majority of this post.

I (ShineBunny) am the moderator of Mafia Game 208. fishy15 is the co-moderator.

Read all of the rules.


Voting and Hangs
  • You must vote using the format of Vote: NAME. The name must be properly spelled (with a notable exception covered below) and everything must be formatted correctly for the vote to count.
  • Votes in hide tags are invalid and will not be counted.
  • You may vote for no hang to occur (thereby skipping the day) by posting Vote: No Hang.
  • Any invalid vote will be completely ignored.
  • For the purposes of voting, the letters I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) will be considered the same; this goes for any other similar looking symbols.
  • If a player receives a majority of the votes, that player will be hanged and the day will end immediately. Day 1 is an exception to this; there is no majority hang on Day 1, which means a hang will only happen at the end of the day.
  • If at least half of the players vote for no hang, the day will end immediately with no hang occurring.
  • If no person receives a majority of the votes by the end of the day, the person with the most votes at that time will be hanged. If there are multiple such people, then a person is randomly hanged from among those with the most votes.
  • As soon as the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted.
  • Votes are locked when there is exactly one more town member alive than mafia.
  • Any incorrect votecounts made will be counted as correct if the mistake is not noticed and corrected within 24 hours.


Activity and Deadlines
  • Each Day phase will be 6 days long.
  • Each Night phase will be 1 day long.
  • Each Day will begin and end at 6:00 PM PDT (UTC-7). If a majority hang causes Day to end early, Night will be extended so that the next Day still begins at this time.
  • I will give out PM Prods at my discretion; there is no official time limit, but I may start looking for a replacement as soon as 24 hours after I give a PM Prod.
  • If you post before I find a replacement (for your inactivity), you will not be replaced.


List of Don’ts
  • Don’t post if you are not participating in this game.
  • Don’t post at night.
  • Don’t talk about this game to anyone except me (the mod) outside of this thread, unless you are given explicit permission by me (the mod) to do so.
  • Don’t edit or delete posts.
  • Don’t conceal text with things such as transparent text and the like. Hide tags are acceptable to use.
  • Don’t post if you are dead.
  • Don’t use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), purple (#8000BF), or anything that resembles one of those colors.
  • Don’t be immature.
  • Don’t gamethrow.
  • Don't ask me (the mod) questions in thread about game mechanics; please ask them in your Role PM, and if I deem it necessary I will publicly clarify the mechanics of the game.
  • Don’t argue with the mod about warnings and modkills.
  • Don’t abuse loopholes.

Violation of any of these rules will result in a warning, a force-replacement, and/or a modkill. I reserve the right to give warnings to, force-replace, and/or modkill anyone for whatever reason. Please do not make me do this.

Warnings
* e_is_cool, Fvr_Vain, mathjay - posting after Day ended
* fdas - posting only twice in an entire Day


Setup
This game will use a modified version of the Pick Your Poison setup. There are significant differences, so don't use that link to figure out how the setup works.
  • There are 13 players.
  • There are 10 Town players, and 3 Mafia players.
  • There is a deadline hang.
  • When there is exactly one more townie than mafia alive, votes become locked.
  • Cops are guaranteed to be sane.
  • Doctors only prevent one night kill.
  • Doctors may self protect, but may not heal the same person two nights in a row.
  • Kills are indistinguishable. There is no difference between a Mafia or a Vigilante kill.
  • Mafia factional kills are both required and assigned. If no factional kill is submitted, the target will be randomized.
  • Mafia can multitask; a Mafia Power Role can both submit the factional kill and another action.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Mafia win when they have parity; that is, when their numbers equal or exceed that of town's.

The game will begin during Night 0. Night 0 will last for 24 hours. Before then, each player will get their alignment. Once alignments have been randomized, Mafia will be able to talk to each other, and during this time and Night 0, they will determine which Power Roles will be in the game. They must select 3 distinct Power Roles for Town from this list: {1-Shot Cop, Doctor, Oracle, Tracker, 2-Shot Vigilante}, and 1 Power Role for Mafia from this list: {Roleblocker, Rolecop}. Note that there is no kill during Night 0. Once the roles have been selected, they will be randomized among their respective factions, players will get their new Role PM, and Day 1 will begin.

Here is a short explanation of each of the roles:
Roles
1-Shot Cop: Once per game at night, you may target a player. You will get their alignment, or be notified that you were roleblocked and received no results.
Doctor: Every night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, that player will be protected from one night kill.
Oracle: Every night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, this player will have been successfully targeted. When you die, your most recent successful target's alignment will be revealed at the start of the Day.
Tracker: Every night, you may target a player. You will receive the name of the player they targeted that night, if any, or that you were roleblocked and received no results.
2-Shot Vigilante: Twice per game at night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, your target will be night killed.

Roleblocker: Every night, you may target a player. That player will be prevented from doing any night actions.
Rolecop: Every night, you may target a player. You will learn their exact role.


Role PMs
Town wrote:
You are Town. Who knows if you'll get some sort of special power? You will receive your full Role PM at the start of Day 1.

You win when all mafia are dead and there is at least one town member alive.

The link to the game thread can be found here.
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Vanilla Townie.

You have no special powers. Your power lies in your voice and your vote.
1-Shot Cop wrote:
You are the 1-Shot Cop.

Once per game at night, you may target a player. You will get their alignment, or be notified that you were roleblocked and received no results.
Doctor wrote:
You are the Doctor.

Every night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, that player will be protected from one night kill.
Oracle wrote:
You are the Oracle.

Every night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, that player will have been successfully targeted. When you die, your most recent successful target's alignment will be revealed at the start of the Day.
Tracker wrote:
You are the Tracker.

Every night, you may target a player. You will receive the name of the player they targeted that night, if any, or that you were roleblocked and received no results.
2-Shot Vigilante wrote:
You are the 2-Shot Vigilante.

Twice per game at night, you may target a player. If you were not roleblocked, your target will be night killed.

Mafia wrote:
You are Mafia. Your teammates are [USERNAME] and [USERNAME]. Who knows if you'll get some sort of special power? You will receive your full Role PM at the start of Day 1.

You may talk with your teammates in this QuickTopic, though other forms of communication (such as Discord) are fine, as long as I (the mod) and the co-mod are in it, as well as all members of the mafia team.

Note that during Night 0, you (and your teammates) must select which Power Roles will be in the game. Refer to the setup for more information, and please submit this in the QuickTopic or whatever form of communication the mafia team decides to use.

You win when mafia's numbers equal or exceed that of town's.

The link to the game thread can be found here.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Mafia Goon. Your teammates are [USERNAME], a Mafia Goon, and [USERNAME], a Mafia Rolecop/Roleblocker.

Every night, a mafia member may submit a factional night kill on a target. If successful, the target player will die.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are a Mafia Roleblocker. Your teammates are [USERNAME] and [USERNAME], both Mafia Goons.

Every night, a mafia member may submit a factional night kill on a target. If successful, the target player will die.

Every night, you may target a player. That player will be prevented from doing any night actions. You can submit both the factional kill and this action in the same night.
Mafia Rolecop wrote:
You are a Mafia Rolecop. Your teammates are [USERNAME] and [USERNAME], both Mafia Goons.
Every night, a mafia member may submit a factional night kill on a target. If successful, the target player will die.

Every night, you may target a player. You will learn their exact role. You can submit both the factional kill and this action in the same night.

A few comments on the other people who have posted:

sudzoof exists.
fdas also exists.
Charge had that interaction with SFGiantsFan, which is weird but I don't really find it necessarily w/w. If one of them flips wolf I could try to dissect every word of it, but I don't feel like it's worth my time doing right now. Charge reads atomicpianowitch as town which I do like.
I like GeneralCobra's reads as a concept. Giving reads is very towny as it allows for just more information flow. Wins in mafia are acquired through teamwork, not by working alone, so in order to solve the game, all townies must work together. And if wolves reveal their thoughts, it becomes much easier to catch them. However, I don't like the null reads. Not one bit. It's understandable this early on, but I do not want ANYONE to have a null read by the end of Day 1.
jeffisepic jumped fairly quickly into the game with a TMI accusation on GeneralCobra19 (who read summitwei as town). I have more on why I don't like these types of TMI accusations later on in this post.
SFGiantsFan's post #27 is another implicit townread on summitwei. I feel like I want to townread someone if lots of people townread them, but like, actually thinking about this, it's just bandwagony and dumb logic. I've actually used this to townread people in like a Cop9er, but that's because n0s are mechanical confirms. Similarly in Avalon, (some) town have mechanical confirms, so you know some of them are right. Meanwhile, in mafia, town is sometimes dumb as bricks, so you can't count on townies being correct.

I initially thought it was helpful, just like in Avalon, but the problem is no one has mechanical information on other people's alignments except for the wolves, at least during Day 1. And it's hard to find like actual w/w interactions this early on. Therefore, a lot of the Avalon theory I've used in the past to try and "confirm" people as town and whatnot goes out the window. TMI is honestly only useful in the case of an actual scumslip. Like if there are two kills at night and mafia assumes a specific one of them is the vigilante shot. (I've made that mistake before in a turbo and got hanged for it.) This is really the only piece of TMI that is reliable. This "oh you read this person as town, thats tmi" is kinda TMI in and of itself if you use that same logic. So screw TMI; I'm going to focus on actual hunting.

The thing is, there's almost no tell of a v/v interaction; in fact, most of it comes down to the lack of other tells, as you mentioned with your pocketing part. This is where I'd have to hard disagree. v/v interaction claims are sketchy at best; you basically just assume two people are town. The thing is, there's almost no tell of a v/v interaction; in fact, most of it comes down to the lack of other tells, as you mentioned with your pocketing part. I find not w/w interactions to be so much more reliable and useful as it's fairly difficult to get them incorrect. Yes, mathjay is tonally town. Yes, Fvr_Vain is being contributive. No, I don't think this clears either of them. It's too easy to get into the confirmation bias habit with interactions, so it feels much safer to just go for no w/w interactions. You assume one of them is a wolf, and try to confirmation bias yourself into thinking the other is a wolf. If you can't, then congrats: that's not a w/w interaction. If you can, then it might be; who knows? I'll probably do some more of this after we get a flip because I'm lazy and don't feel like doing this on a site with a terrible ISO system (ie none).

Gun to Head Reads

This is usually done in a live setting, but AoPS being as inactive as it is, I guess I'll make do with a non-live version.

so youre reading on aggressiveness now
?
?
?

post_rendered:
Okay I'm not going to bother waiting for fdas to do this; he can pop in with an actual GtH readslist in the future if he would like to. I'm putting him as reading atomicpianowitch as mafia and everyone else as town.

First, the raw data:
Chart


Reads by Players
The first thing I noticed is the staggering consistency in the number of people each player scumreads. In a normal GtH, this would actually be done one player at a time, and would be even more spontaneous, but people like Fvr_Vain already mentioned how they scumread 4 people when there's only 3 mafia. That's fine; no one is ever certain in their reads. However, I do find it significant when players scumread either very few, or very many people. Scumreading very few people could be an indication of a mafioso not wanting to ruffle feathers, and scumreading many people could be an indication of a mafioso wanting to keep the PoE wide.

So, I have questions directed towards fdas, mathjay, and tikkitamatoa. All of you scumread 3 or less people. Why so? Are you confident enough in your few reads? And, do you think the 3 (or, in the case of fdas, one) people you scumread is the exact scumteam?

And, conversely, Charge has scumread 5 people. She also put question marks and stuff when I explicitly said not to do so… Anyways, talk to me about your reads. I don't think I've seen Charge being that active in pushing people, even though she suspects so many. Her posts have been more thought dumps rather than actual pushes.


Reads on Players
Now, based on the number of townreads each person got, I will categorize them into a few categories:

Consensus Town: e_is_cool, jeffisepic, mathjay
Both me and jeffisepic being here are expected, but mathjay being here is interesting, especially after I've seen multiple people (which, if I recall correctly, includes players such as Charge and atomicpianowitch) attack mathjay. I could see this going one of two ways:
mathjay is mafia, and her his teammates wanted to distance, yet not hang him.
mathjay is town, and mafia, after doing some probing attacks, decided he was too hard to hang.
For the people who have attacked mathjay at some point, you have clearly changed your mind. Why so? (Mostly directed at Charge and atomicpianowitch, though there may be some others I forgot.)

Mostly Townread: Charge, Fvr_Vain, NDMath, SFGiantsFan, summitwei
Honestly, the one I'm most surprised to see here is NDMath, and to a lesser extent SFGiantsFan.
Specifically, NDMath being scumread by three people: atomicpianowitch, sudzoof, and tikkitamatoa. Of note is that atomicpianowitch scumreads the other two. atomicpianowitch, do you feel comfortable sharing this read with half of your scumreads?
Regardless, NDMath has been sorta a low poster, and many people reading him as town would make sense if NDMath was scum. After all, I know mafia tried to make the number of scumreads they had be a reasonable amount, and if mafia didn't want to actually scumread their teammates, they'd have to scumread a townie. A low poster is usually a good cop out option, yet NDMath wasn't that scumread.
This same logic could apply to SFGiantsFan, though to a lesser extent as he had one more scumread on him than NDMath. However, there's a key difference here. SFGiantsFan has been scumread by the more towny people, and NDMath has been scumread by the more scummy people. I personally think this points to NDMath being more likely town over SFGiantsFan.

Mixed: atomicpianowitch, fdas, GeneralCobra19, tikkitamatoa
Absolutely no one here surprises me. I don't think I can get much out of this without reaching.

sudzoof: sudzoof
This is the sudzoof tier. The almost universal scumread, who is scumread by everyone except fdas (who didn't even follow the instructions) and tikkitamatoa. Who, before my GtH exercise post, had almost no wagon on them; just light pressure. I agree with what most people have said: this is the slot that almost everyone scumreads, but no one has really pushed them. This reads as wolves acknowledging that their partner is scummy, yet don't really want them to die. I also think I've actually seen this exact archetype being described in an MU article as almost always being a wolf. Vote: sudzoof

Vote: sudzoof

Other Thoughts
Of course, there's more to this chart than just what I pointed out, but those were some of the overall picture stuff I found, and everyone is free to use this in their own analysis. In fact, I would encourage people to look at this to try and back up any analysis they may have.

Something I did briefly touch up on was people who had similar scumreads to their scumreads. This is usually a sign of distancing between wolves that have the same agenda. Scumread each other for the distance, yet still try and push the same targets as a whole. However, I am too dumb with technology to be able to analyze this in an automated way, so I have mostly been doing this by hand.

Speaking of shared reads, I did notice that Charge and jeffisepic have 4 shared scumreads. I don't think that's really significant, as the only conclusion you can really try and draw from this is "Charge may be sheeping jeffisepic" which isn't very convincing. This might be important in light of future analysis though.

Mutual scumreads were also something I looked at. Players who scumread each other. In addition, players who are being scumread but DON'T scumread the other person back. Obviously, this latter thing happens mostly to the people who are scumread, as they just have more people on their tail. However strange this sounds, I find that mafia tend to have a harder time scumreading people who attack them, as they know that their attacker is town (unless it's a bus, then whatever), while town have to justify townreading their attacker, as the natural instinct is to OMGUS. The specific person I noticed this in is atomicpianowitch, who townreads four of the five people scumreading him.

And finally, it should be pretty obvious that mafia tried to coordinate their reads, since this wasn't a live exercise. Now, I have no idea how mafia did this, but it's nonetheless important to keep in mind.

Remember, this chart is just a pile of statistics; it doesn't mean much by itself. But if people actually analyze it, and use it in conjunction with other tools, it can be really helpful.


tl;dr

kinda like ...
this

I had been focusing a lot on ND, vain, e/jeff, summit, looking at apw and mathjay, but I think I didn’t notice sud until whoever voted him first. Because town should always hang, I pegged my vote on him because I couldn’t see an ND hang happening and I wasn’t going to be able to push one. By EOD I was genuinely convinced that sud was scum and honestly I was surprised by his flip but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

I have a strong suspicion that there is scum on the main push on sud - I didn’t think there was quite enough info at the beginning (before sud started ‘defending’) to read him strongly as a wolf
This post basically says "I thought sudzoof was town but I didn't want to defend him because I was afraid that if I did it too hard sudzoof wouldn't actually be hanged." It's important to note that the counter-wagon at this time was on NDMath.

post_rendered:
Conclusions!!
- there was an insane amount of BWing from both town and scum, maybe everyone's sheeping jeff or something but thats not ok.
- I am deeply suspicious of vain for reading sud as scum in his GTH (an easy slip to make if you're tunneling 2 people and need to find another that wouldn't be too controversial). However, I think it's worth noting that he also GTH'd GC as scum. I don't think this says much - GC had been acting pretty darn scummy in hindsight. he also is very self-concious about it. "wait oops I put 4 scum but theres 4 scummy people so whatever". Why the meganormous flip from sud town to sud scummy, and then refusing to vote on the sud wagon? Note that there were no logical transitions between these states that he expressed in-thread. I want to do a vain ISO.
- I am deeply suspicious of ND because of the way and the timing with which he jumped on the sud wagon, especially with GC's flip in context. He also lurks a lot, as sud mentioned. I want to do an ND iso.
- apw gets a scumlean from me.
- SFGF is town for me because of post 320 and 340, where he pushes GC HARD. scum has literally no reason to do this and i'm inclined to believe scum!SFGF, if attempting to earn towncred, wouldnt have done it that way.

Regarding NDMath specifically:
I was expecting to write why I scumread NDMath, but looking at his posts a little closer I really don't scumread him anymore.
One of the major arguments I had before was that NDMath's play wasn't matching up with his town meta: in 191 and 200, he played pretty hard and was the style of play that got him MVP. But 1. NDMath explained this, he thought he was third party in 200 and in 191 it was largely mechanical and 2. I forgot about 197, which I modded, and where he played quite similar to how he's playing now.
NDMath had 14 posts this D1, which isn't really a lot. But he had 31 posts on D1 of 197, and considering that that D1 had 1500 posts and was twice as long time-wise, I think those numbers largely match up. In fact, in one of his posts that he made, he said "Oops I'm lurking".
Additionally, his posts largely consisted of 1. responding to things via quotes and 2. giving unexplained but confident reads, which matches up with this game perfectly. #128 from this game has the unexplained SFGF vote, as well as responding to quotes. In #244 he drops a tiered readlist with no explanations, which matches up with #1444 from 197.

Plus, his D2 has been largely towny as well (there's not much but there's some):
-There's no cracking under pressure, or any sense of opportunism, despite the fact that he's one of the lead candidates atm
-The Charge/GC analysis makes sense, and is only incriminating at all if Charge is scum
-The me/e scumteam idea is original, and feels like a random town thought
-The rest of his posts line up with his town meta as described earlier

The only thing left is his jump on the sud wagon, and I would argue even that isn't as scummy as people say. His defense in #533 largely lines up with my own progression on sud, in that he'd given a slight scumread on sud a while back, and so the vote wasn't out of the blue. So I'm not going to scumread him for pretty much the same thing I did.

Hopefully I will feel motivated to look closer at vain and tikki later, because looking at ND was actually pretty fun and I feel like I understand the game now. Gonna go play minecraft bye
